LOCAL GOVERNMENT ROUNDUP

Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee moved updated nuisance rules toward adoption and flagged a busy agenda for the village board, while the Wausau School Board was set to take up academic standards and building repairs Monday evening.

  • Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee unanimously forwarded revised Chapter 382 Nuisances ordinance language to the Village Board for approval at its July 6 meeting.
  • The CLIPP Committee reached consensus to ask Marathon County to conduct a traffic study at County Road XX and Terabonding Drive, with the existing four-way stop to remain in place.
  • Kronenwetter staff was directed to research costs and grant options for a bike and pedestrian path along Highway X, with a report due back to the committee in August.
  • The Kronenwetter Village Board is set to consider ballot drop box installation, revisions to Chapter 41 Elections, and oak wilt regulations under Chapter 389 at its Monday meeting.
  • The Wausau School Board is set to vote Monday on adopting Wisconsin Academic Standards, renewing WIAA memberships for Wausau East, Wausau West, and WAVE, and approving an EFIS wall repair at South Mountain Elementary.

Coming up: The Wausau Common Council meets Tuesday at 6:30 p.m., preceded by Finance Committee at 5:15 p.m. and Board of Public Works at 10:30 a.m.; Marathon County's Public Safety Committee also convenes Tuesday at 5:00 p.m.
